steps.title: Identify the strongest clue
steps.content: The plan is described as “excellent” and “simply arranged,” but the narrator immediately adds: “the only difficulty was, that Alice had not the smallest idea how to set about it.” This contrast reveals that the plan was straightforward or easy to grasp in concept, even though Alice couldn't actually execute it.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content: ”Simply” must mean “in a straightforward, uncomplicated manner.” Easily expresses this exactly: an easily-arranged plan is one that's clear and uncomplicated. The other choices don't fit—they denote speed, weakness, or foolishness, qualities unrelated to describing a plan's straightforwardness. That matches choice C — C is correct.
